ref_source: REF, "UK Renewable Electricity Subsidy Totals 2002 to the Present Day", April 2025, Tables 1 and 2

components.note: Both series sum the same April-March Ofgem scheme years, so the gap is not a calendar-vs-fiscal timing difference. Ours uses Ofgem's published FIT scheme value (generation + export + qualifying costs); REF's lower figure appears to use a narrower payments basis, plus rounding to GBP 0.1bn. The residual is a definitional difference and is not yet fully reconciled with REF.

REF's CfD line covers renewable generators including biomass; ours is CfD-renewables, which likewise already includes biomass (Drax and the like). CfD-nuclear contributes nothing in this window - Hinkley Point C payments begin in 2025.
REGO certificate costs are excluded from the Clock entirely (listed under "What is not included").
REF counts total CCL receipts (OBR series, all fuels); we count electricity's share of main-rate receipts plus Carbon Price Support.
REF does not net off direct constraint payments; we do, to avoid double-counting against our constraints line.
REF counts total ETS receipts (OBR series, all sectors); we count only the power-sector share of auctioned allowance revenue.
